name: Irish Soda Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup raisins (add a few more if desired)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the bread flour, sugar, salt, butter (or canola oil), raisins, and caraway seeds.
2. Make a well in the center of the mixture and add the baking soda and buttermilk.
3. Stir until a shaggy dough forms, then stir in the dry yeast.
4. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead briefly to bring it together.
5. Form the dough into a round loaf and place it on a greased baking sheet.
6. Cover and let rise for 30-45 minutes, or until nearly doubled in size.
7. Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
8. Place the loaf on a greased baking sheet and cover with a towel.
9. Let rise again for another 15-20 minutes.
10. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
11. Bake the bread for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and hollow-sounding when tapped.
12. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack before slicing.
